artifact.transformation_rule: Source R workflow: optional X-13 seasonal adjustment when flagged, then log-difference over non-missing observations; first three rows are dropped so the estimation sample starts at settings.R start_date.
artifact.missing_value_rule: Missing observations are retained as NA in the ragged-edge panel. Growth is computed only across non-missing observations, then written back to the original positions; leading/ending all-NA rows are removed before EM estimation and the Kalman filter smooths remaining gaps.
artifact.production_note: This artifact documents the current source workflow and proposes row-level owner-review decisions before a production refit. It is not a claim that every source transformation has model-owner sign-off.

variables.source_header: GDP real in Bil. 2021 UZS, volume terms
variables.label: Uzbekistan, Gross Domestic Product, Total, Constant Prices, volume 2021, UZS
variables.transformation: Source R workflow: optional X-13 seasonal adjustment when flagged, then log-difference over non-missing observations; first three rows are dropped so the estimation sample starts at settings.R start_date.
variables.source_workflow_confirmation: Confirmed in main.R -> prepare_data.R -> calculate_growth.R: the current source workflow applies the same log-difference rule to every variable after optional X-13 seasonal adjustment.
variables.recommended_transformation: Quarterly GDP level -> log quarter-on-quarter growth for model input; postprocess predicted QoQ path into GDP levels and YoY percent.
variables.rationale: GDP is the quarterly target level, so log QoQ growth is economically meaningful for the state-space model; published YoY growth should come only from the GDP post-processing step.
variables.public_display_guidance: No as a top high-frequency contribution: GDP is the target row and should be excluded from indicator-contribution diagnostics.
variables.missing_value_rule: Missing observations are retained as NA in the ragged-edge panel. Growth is computed only across non-missing observations, then written back to the original positions; leading/ending all-NA rows are removed before EM estimation and the Kalman filter smooths remaining gaps.
variables.contribution_guardrail: No as a top high-frequency contribution: GDP is the target row and should be excluded from indicator-contribution diagnostics.

variables.source_header: Uzbekistan, Industrial Production, Total, Total Industrial Output, CPPY=100, Index
variables.label: Industrial Production, Total, Total Industrial Output, CPPY=100, Index
variables.source: Statistics Agency under the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan
variables.transformation: Source R workflow: optional X-13 seasonal adjustment when flagged, then log-difference over non-missing observations; first three rows are dropped so the estimation sample starts at settings.R start_date.
variables.source_workflow_confirmation: Confirmed in main.R -> prepare_data.R -> calculate_growth.R: the current source workflow applies the same log-difference rule to every variable after optional X-13 seasonal adjustment.
variables.recommended_transformation: Treat as an already year-on-year index: use log(index / 100) or index minus 100 as the growth signal; do not log-difference the index again.
variables.rationale: CPPY=100 means the observation already compares output with the corresponding period of the previous year. Differencing its log measures acceleration of the YoY rate, not industrial-output growth.
variables.public_display_guidance: Yes for internal preview: contribution is a standardized common-factor signal and must not be read as a GDP percentage-point effect.
variables.missing_value_rule: Missing observations are retained as NA in the ragged-edge panel. Growth is computed only across non-missing observations, then written back to the original positions; leading/ending all-NA rows are removed before EM estimation and the Kalman filter smooths remaining gaps.
variables.contribution_guardrail: Yes for internal preview: contribution is a standardized common-factor signal and must not be read as a GDP percentage-point effect.

variables.label: Consumer Price Index, Services, Total, PP=100, Index
variables.source: Statistics Agency under the President of the Republic of Uzbekistan
variables.transformation: Source R workflow: optional X-13 seasonal adjustment when flagged, then log-difference over non-missing observations; first three rows are dropped so the estimation sample starts at settings.R start_date.
variables.source_workflow_confirmation: Confirmed in main.R -> prepare_data.R -> calculate_growth.R: the current source workflow applies the same log-difference rule to every variable after optional X-13 seasonal adjustment.
variables.recommended_transformation: Use log-difference of the positive PP=100 price index to measure monthly services inflation.
variables.rationale: A CPI index is a price level. Log-differencing a positive price index produces an inflation-rate signal suitable for a stationary DFM input.

variables.source_header: Uzbekistan, Financial Soundness Indicators, Banking Sector, Nonperforming Loans to Total Gross Loans
variables.label: Financial Soundness Indicators, Banking Sector, Nonperforming Loans to Total Gross Loans
variables.source: Central Bank of the Republic of Uzbekistan
variables.transformation: Source R workflow: optional X-13 seasonal adjustment when flagged, then log-difference over non-missing observations; first three rows are dropped so the estimation sample starts at settings.R start_date.
variables.source_workflow_confirmation: Confirmed in main.R -> prepare_data.R -> calculate_growth.R: the current source workflow applies the same log-difference rule to every variable after optional X-13 seasonal adjustment.
variables.recommended_transformation: Use the NPL ratio as a monthly percentage-point change for model input; keep the ratio level only for descriptive diagnostics.
variables.rationale: The nonperforming-loan share is a financial ratio, not a quantity level. A percentage-point change is interpretable as credit-quality deterioration or improvement, while log-changing the ratio can overstate movements when the ratio is low.
variables.public_display_guidance: Yes for internal preview: contribution is a standardized common-factor signal and must not be read as a GDP percentage-point effect.
variables.missing_value_rule: Missing observations are retained as NA in the ragged-edge panel. Growth is computed only across non-missing observations, then written back to the original positions; leading/ending all-NA rows are removed before EM estimation and the Kalman filter smooths remaining gaps.
variables.contribution_guardrail: Yes for internal preview: contribution is a standardized common-factor signal and must not be read as a GDP percentage-point effect.

variables.source_header: Uzbekistan, Deposits & Loans, All Institutions, Interest Rates on Deposits, By Currency, In Local Currency, Total, Over 1 Year, Weighted Average Rate
variables.label: Uzbekistan, Deposits & Loans, All Institutions, Interest Rates on Deposits, By Currency, In Local Currency, Total, Over 1 Year, Weighted Average Rate
variables.source: Central Bank of the Republic of Uzbekistan
variables.transformation: Source R workflow: optional X-13 seasonal adjustment when flagged, then log-difference over non-missing observations; first three rows are dropped so the estimation sample starts at settings.R start_date.
variables.source_workflow_confirmation: Confirmed in main.R -> prepare_data.R -> calculate_growth.R: the current source workflow applies the same log-difference rule to every variable after optional X-13 seasonal adjustment.
variables.recommended_transformation: Use the month-to-month percentage-point change in the 1-year deposit rate for the DFM input; keep the rate level for interpretation.
variables.rationale: Interest rates are already measured in percent per year. A percentage-point change captures monetary and funding-cost news directly; a log-change of the rate itself is not a standard macro-financial signal.
variables.public_display_guidance: Yes for internal preview: contribution is a standardized common-factor signal and must not be read as a GDP percentage-point effect.
variables.missing_value_rule: Missing observations are retained as NA in the ragged-edge panel. Growth is computed only across non-missing observations, then written back to the original positions; leading/ending all-NA rows are removed before EM estimation and the Kalman filter smooths remaining gaps.
variables.contribution_guardrail: Yes for internal preview: contribution is a standardized common-factor signal and must not be read as a GDP percentage-point effect.

variables.source_header: Uzbekistan, FX Spot Rates, Central Bank of The Republic of Uzbekistan, UZS per USD
variables.label: FX Spot Rates, Central Bank of The Republic of Uzbekistan, UZS per USD
variables.source: Central Bank of the Republic of Uzbekistan
variables.transformation: Source R workflow: optional X-13 seasonal adjustment when flagged, then log-difference over non-missing observations; first three rows are dropped so the estimation sample starts at settings.R start_date.
variables.source_workflow_confirmation: Confirmed in main.R -> prepare_data.R -> calculate_growth.R: the current source workflow applies the same log-difference rule to every variable after optional X-13 seasonal adjustment.
variables.recommended_transformation: Aggregate weekly UZS/USD observations to a monthly average, then use the log monthly change in UZS per USD as depreciation/appreciation news.
variables.rationale: The exchange-rate level can be transformed with log changes, but the source frequency is weekly. A monthly average gives a stable monthly signal and avoids making the DFM depend on a single end-of-month quote.
variables.public_display_guidance: Yes for internal preview: contribution is a standardized common-factor signal and must not be read as a GDP percentage-point effect.
variables.missing_value_rule: Missing observations are retained as NA in the ragged-edge panel. Growth is computed only across non-missing observations, then written back to the original positions; leading/ending all-NA rows are removed before EM estimation and the Kalman filter smooths remaining gaps.
variables.contribution_guardrail: Yes for internal preview: contribution is a standardized common-factor signal and must not be read as a GDP percentage-point effect.

variables.source_header: Kazakhstan, Leading Indicators, Bureau of National Statistics of the Agency for Strategic Planning & Reforms, Short-Term Economic Indicator, Change YTD PY=100, Index
variables.label: Kazakhstan, Leading Indicators, Bureau of National Statistics of the Agency for Strategic Planning & Reforms, Short-Term Economic Indicator, Change YTD PY=100, Index
variables.source: Kazakh Agency of Statistics
variables.transformation: Source R workflow: optional X-13 seasonal adjustment when flagged, then log-difference over non-missing observations; first three rows are dropped so the estimation sample starts at settings.R start_date.
variables.source_workflow_confirmation: Confirmed in main.R -> prepare_data.R -> calculate_growth.R: the current source workflow applies the same log-difference rule to every variable after optional X-13 seasonal adjustment.
variables.recommended_transformation: Use index minus 100 as a YTD-over-previous-year foreign-activity signal; do not log-difference the cumulative YTD index.
variables.rationale: The source is a change YTD PY=100 index, so it is already a cumulative growth comparison. Using the deviation from 100 preserves the foreign-activity signal; log-differencing would mix monthly revisions with changes in the YTD comparison window.
variables.public_display_guidance: Yes for internal preview: contribution is a standardized common-factor signal and must not be read as a GDP percentage-point effect.
variables.missing_value_rule: Missing observations are retained as NA in the ragged-edge panel. Growth is computed only across non-missing observations, then written back to the original positions; leading/ending all-NA rows are removed before EM estimation and the Kalman filter smooths remaining gaps.

variables.contribution_guardrail: Yes for internal preview: contribution is a standardized common-factor signal and must not be read as a GDP percentage-point effect.

variables.label: business clmate index
variables.source: CERR
variables.transformation: Source R workflow: optional X-13 seasonal adjustment when flagged, then log-difference over non-missing observations; first three rows are dropped so the estimation sample starts at settings.R start_date.
variables.source_workflow_confirmation: Confirmed in main.R -> prepare_data.R -> calculate_growth.R: the current source workflow applies the same log-difference rule to every variable after optional X-13 seasonal adjustment.
variables.recommended_transformation: Use the business-climate index in levels or first differences after owner confirmation of the survey scale; avoid treating it as a physical quantity level.
variables.rationale: Survey climate indexes are sentiment measures. They can help the factor, but the scale and zero point should be documented before a production refit.
